title: Tephrosia decaryana
taxonomy: Tephrosia decaryana is classified in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. It belongs to the genus Tephrosia, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The family Fabaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, and Tephrosia decaryana falls under the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Tephrosia is also known for its diverse range of species, many of which are used in traditional medicine. Further classification of Tephrosia decaryana may also involve its species rank within the genus.

It is also reported to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it potentially useful in treating infections. Further studies are necessary to fully understand and harness the medicinal potential of Tephrosia decaryana.

intro: Tephrosia decaryana, commonly known as the "Yellow Pea Tree," is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to Madagascar. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Tephrosia decaryana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
